t_ch: 看你这老不死的样子…啊我明白了，你是为了不老不死吧？
t_en: You practically have one foot in the grave... Don't tell me you're going to wish for immortality.

t_ch: 我已经活过了十个琥珀纪，只要我愿意，我的医疗团队努努力甚至可以让我再多活一阵……
t_en: Oh, I've lived for ten Amber Eras already. As long as I give the word, my medical team can squeeze a few more years out of these old bones.

t_ch: 难不成是为了消灭自己的对手？听说，你和我的御主之间可是有些芥蒂。
t_en: Then are you trying to crush your business rivals? I heard there's some bad blood between you and my Master.

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬嗬！这可是天大的误会，商场之中从来不存在什么绝对的敌人！
t_en: Ohohoho! What a great misunderstanding! There are no enemies that can't be made into friends in the world of business!

t_ch: 这话说的倒也不错，只是，我对老奥帝先生的盘算也很感兴趣。
t_en: Good point, but I'm still very curious about your little scheme.

t_ch: 所以，老奥帝先生，你该不会像我所知的那些魔术师一样，为了追寻一个抵达万物根源的可能，连生命和人性都可以放弃。
t_en: Mr. Oti, surely you wouldn't be like the mages I know, willing to forsake both life and humanity just to reach the root of all things?

t_ch: …这位小姐，你所说的事情，只有天才俱乐部的家伙才会追逐不休。
t_en: ...Miss, what you're talking about is something only the Genius Society would obsessively chase after.

t_ch: 所以到底是为什么？
t_en: What's your real reason, then?

t_ch: 总不可能是为了钱吧？
t_en: It couldn't possibly be for money, could it?

t_ch: 哦嚯嚯嚯，为什么不能？
t_en: Hahaha... why not?

t_ch: 我从一开始就说得明明白白。谐乐大典草草落幕，而匹诺康尼需要全新的经济增长点，我们得打造一个足够引人注目的商业项目！
t_en: I've been very clear about this from the start. The Charmony Festival met a hasty wrap, and Penacony needs a fresh economic boost. We need to create a business venture that will grab everyone's attention!

t_ch: 而「能够实现愿望的圣杯」就是这只会下金蛋的鹅！一旦它举办成功，匹诺康尼将会拥有一项足以令其再兴盛十个琥珀纪的伟大庆典，连谐乐大典也无法与之媲美！
t_en: A Holy Grail that grants wishes is just the golden goose we need! If this ritual works, then Penacony will own a glorious show to keep the money rolling in for ten more Amber Eras, a carnival that even the Charmony Festival can't compete with!

t_ch: 当然，在这前无古人的匹诺康尼第一届圣杯战争下，我也有一些属于自己的私人小目标…但这就和诸位没有关系了。
t_en: Even if I do have some personal objectives in this first-ever Penacony Holy Grail War... that's none of your concern.

t_ch: 向圣杯要钱不行吗？
t_en: Why don't you just ask the Holy Grail for cash?

t_ch: 凭空出现的财富只会引起通货膨胀！金融的秘密可不是靠空口许愿就能实现的！那需要精妙的平衡与操作。我喜欢钱，但我不喜欢一味增长的数字游戏！
t_en: Wealth that pops out of thin air will only lead to inflation! Financial growth can't be achieved by miracles. It requires precise balance and expert handling. I like money, but I don't like to play a numbers game.

t_ch: 这也太庸俗了吧！
t_en: What a crude wish!

t_ch: 我喜欢看人们蜂拥挤入匹诺康尼，用自己的全部家财去换一个加入圣杯战争的机会！我喜欢苜蓿币从「烫金的时刻」流淌出去，注入银河的市场买入卖出！
t_en: What I like is watching people swarm into Penacony, spending their entire fortunes just to buy a chance to enter the Holy Grail War! I like having Alfalfa credits pour out of the Gilded Hour, entering the Cosmic Exchange!

t_ch: 我喜欢看见敌人被各种手段揉扁搓圆，最后血本无归！我喜欢将那些连名字都没听过的企业大笔一挥收购，成为我巨大商业版图的一部分！
t_en: I like seeing my business rivals being crushed and manipulated, and then lose everything in the end! I like to acquire companies that I've never heard of, fusing them into my empire with a mere wave of my pen!

t_ch: 「黄金的时刻」要看我的脸色给商品定价，「蓝调的时刻」为我制定匹诺康尼的时尚潮流。
t_en: The Golden Hour will set the prices for goods based on my desires, and the Blue Hour will craft Penaconian fashion based on my whims.

t_ch: 梦想？愿望？你们真以为匹诺康尼建立在这些虚无缥缈的东西上？不！支撑它的，恰恰是你们眼中最庸俗的东西，是源源不断的货币！金钱，才是这盛会之星最坚固的基石！
t_en: Do you honestly believe Penacony was built on empty ideas like "dreams" and "wishes"? No! What truly underpins and supports this city is what you deem "crude." It's the unending flow of money! On this rock called money, we have built the Planet of Festivities!

t_ch: 不管你作何打算，「圣杯战争」也只会决出一位胜者。而你在这场闹剧中的所作所为，已经让所有从者都站在了自己的对立面。
t_en: No matter what your plans may be, there can only be one victor in the Holy Grail War. Your actions in this farce have already placed every Servant squarely against you.

t_ch: 现在，我们已经站到你的面前了，精明的商人。
t_en: Now, we stand together before you, shrewd merchant.

t_ch: 我只想把几小时前没办到的事继续做下去——鱼头我已经看厌了，这愿望落空的、不甘心的、老不死的头，那才叫上等的战利品呢！
t_en: I just wanna finish what I didn't get to do a few hours ago. I'm sick and tired of fish heads — The head of this old desperate fogey with broken dreams is what I'd call a real trophy!

t_ch: Lancer，你把这位老先生满脑子坏水的脑袋当战利品，标准实在太低了些。
t_en: You see this evil man's old head as a trophy? I thought you had better taste than that, Lancer.

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬嗬！看来各位急着为我安排后事了。不过，既然这场游戏只能有一位赢家，即使我败退了，你们又要如何决出胜负呢？
t_en: Ohohoho! It seems you're all eager to prepare for my demise. But since there can only be one winner in this game, how do you plan to decide the victor after I bow out?

t_ch: 所以来谈谈合作如何？无论是谁，只要能将其他人排除，我就把向圣杯许愿的资格让渡给他。
t_en: Why don't we discuss... a collaboration instead? Whoever can eliminate the others shall have the chance to make their wish to the Holy Grail. I will give you that right.

t_ch: 我们甚至可以签订一张合同，用定义精确、逻辑干净的语言许愿，让圣杯满足所有人的愿望——各位御主和从者，你们为之战斗的不就是这一刻吗？
t_en: We can even formalize it with a contract — using precise, logical language to word our wishes, ensuring the Holy Grail fulfills everyone's desires... After all, isn't this the very moment you've all been fighting for, Masters and Servants?

t_ch: 嘘，不可以和解哦。
t_en: Shh. No peaceful resolutions, please.

t_ch: 事到如今？你在搞笑吗？
t_en: This far into the show? Are you trying to make me laugh?

t_ch: 以玷污所有人愿望为代价的圣杯，不过是个该被打碎的假货。我说的没错吧，御主？
t_en: A Holy Grail that sullies everyone's wishes is just a counterfeit for us to smash to pieces. Isn't that right, Master?

t_ch: 是先大闹一场，再将这假货打得粉碎才对啊。
t_en: Nah. We need to whip this fella's hind-end, THEN smash it to pieces.

t_ch: 难得我们意见一致，我挺喜欢这方案的。你怎么说，砂金先生？偶尔当一次正义的伙伴也不错吧？
t_en: Our opinions rarely align, but I'm fond of this idea. What do you think, Mr. Aventurine? It's nice to play as the hero of justice now and then, isn't it?

t_ch: 我今天已经充当过一次众矢之的了啊…这一次，机会就留给老奥帝先生好了。
t_en: I've already been the scapegoat once today... So I'll pass this opportunity to Mr. Oti.

t_ch: 御主，看来我没法为你夺得圣杯了。
t_en: My Master, it seems I won't be able to claim the Holy Grail for you.

t_ch: 我相信你的选择。
t_en: I trust your choice.

t_ch: 你的选择是正确的。
t_en: You made the right choice.

t_ch: 这是我们共同的决定！
t_en: We made this choice together.

t_ch: 要多谢音符小姐那场梦境，让我看得更清楚了…<color=#dbc291ff>重要的是为了拯救什么、追逐什么而行走至今的过程</color>。
t_en: I must thank Miss Note's dream for showing me the truth... <color=#dbc291ff>What truly matters is not the ending, but the journey we've taken to chase something and to deliver it from destruction</color>.

t_ch: 我不需要圣杯，我没有实现的愿望也将在此作结。
t_en: I don't need the Holy Grail. The wishes I did not fulfill will end here.

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬嗬…各位执意要将矛头对准我，那我只好勉为其难充当这场节目的关底考验了。
t_en: Ohohoho... If you are all so keen to make me your enemy, I suppose I'll have to become the final challenge on this variety show.

t_ch: 在加入圣杯战争时，御主应当召唤一位历史名人作为从者…这是定例。但我该召唤谁呢？这个问题让我为难了许久……
t_en: The rules said a Master is supposed to summon a historical figure as their Servant to enter the Holy Grail War, but... who should I summon? This question has haunted me for quite some time...

t_ch: 是被我资助的落魄导演？还是被我买走了名字的传奇明星？不不，这些统统都不够格……
t_en: Should I get the washed-up director I sponsored? Or the legendary star whose name I purchased? No. No. No. They are not worthy of being my Servant...

t_ch: 有个念头一闪而过…为什么我不召唤我自己呢？一个历经十个琥珀纪的争斗，如今依然屹立不倒的传奇人物，不就在镜子里吗？
t_en: That's when a thought crossed my mind... Why not summon myself? A person who's still standing strong after ten Amber Eras of business intrigue... Isn't this living legend staring back at me in the mirror?

t_ch: 所以，我以自己为召唤触媒…想看看投射在匹诺康尼历史中的商业巨头奥帝，会以怎样一番从者的形象回应我的召唤？在历史中人们到底是如何看待我的？
t_en: I used myself as the catalyst... I wanted to see how the business magnate Oti, reflected in the history of Penacony, would respond to my call as a Servant. I wanted to see how people truly view me in history.

t_ch: 你有答案了吗？
t_en: And have you got your answer?

t_ch: 噗…哼哼，哈哈哈哈哈哈——
t_en: Pfft... Hehehe, hahahahahaha—

t_ch: 答案伤人啊。在他们眼里，我不过是一只，趴在匹诺康尼的橡树顶端——
t_en: The answer really hurts. To them, I'm nothing but a parasite, sucking out the sap of Penacony's oak from the top —

t_ch: <size=36><color=#dbc291ff>为了金钱而疯狂啃噬的蛀虫</color></size>。
t_en: <size=36><color=#dbc291ff>A bug madly devouring wealth.</color></size>

t_ch: （波提欧他们也出来了，要不去看看他们的状况？）
t_en: (Boothill and the others got out too. Should I go check on them?)

t_ch: 夺取圣杯也好，碾碎圣杯也罢！能办到的话，就来吧！
t_en: Do you want to take the Holy Grail, or crush it!? You'll have to get through me first.

t_ch: 所以打一开始在这儿迎击我们的，就是老奥帝所召唤的从者吗？
t_en: So the person we've been talking to was Old Oti's Servant all along?

t_ch: 让从者面对我们，自己却居身幕后，真是稳健的战略……
t_en: Letting his Servant meet us, while he stays safely behind the scenes... What a calculating strategy...

t_ch: 必须把圣杯解体，否则，所有人都会被这群虫子拖垮。
t_en: We must break the Holy Grail, or we'll lose in this war of attrition against these pests.

t_ch: 那好，清理虫子就交给你了。一骑当千可算是王者的特长吧？
t_en: I'll leave the bug extermination to you then. Kings are good at facing down entire armies on their own, aren't they?

t_ch: 终于，这一切都结束了……
t_en: It's finally over...

t_ch: 多灾多难的大剧院，我都有些心疼匹诺康尼的猎犬和筑梦师了。
t_en: Is this Grand Theater ever gonna see a day of peace...? I'm starting to feel bad for the Bloodhounds and the Dreamweavers.

t_ch: 不必为了一栋被保上了几百种保险的古老建筑物操心…老奥帝这家伙从来不会在钱的问题上让自己吃亏。
t_en: No need to feel bad for a heritage building insured with hundreds of different policies. Old Oti would never allow himself to lose a single coin over that.

t_ch: 哼，把所有人都卷进了这场「圣杯战争」里，自己却躲在幕后，难道没人打算去找他讨个说法？
t_en: So he dragged everyone into this Holy Grail War while hiding in the shadows, and none of you are gonna make him pay for it?

t_ch: 狠狠地揍他！
t_en: We should give him a good thrashing!

t_ch: 赔偿精神损失费！
t_en: Make him pay for our emotional damage!

t_ch: 很遗憾，没有任何一位御主在这次事件中受伤，就算是向他问责，怕是也只会被他用他最熟悉的两样手段——最顶尖的律师团队，还有天价的赔偿给糊弄过去。
t_en: Unfortunately, no Master was harmed in the making of this show. Even if we tried to hold him accountable, he'd probably just brush it off with his usual tricks — the best legal team money can buy and sky-high compensation.

t_ch: 而「圣杯战争」的举行，显然已经让他得到了远超这些成本的收益。
t_en: And... I fear the Holy Grail War has already given him returns far beyond what he's put into it.

t_ch: 不过在谈判场上，这场闹剧会是个好用的借口，为翡翠女士在匹诺康尼撬开更大的入口。
t_en: The silver lining is that this little spectacle will serve as the perfect excuse during negotiations for Madam Jade to pry open an even bigger opportunity in Penacony.

t_ch: 斯科特没有人权吗？
t_en: Does Skott have no rights?

t_ch: 斯科特先生的麻烦当然该由公司同僚来操心才是。
t_en: Mr. Skott's issues will be handled by the IPC.

t_ch: 从者们没有人权吗？
t_en: Do Servants have no rights?

t_ch: 很遗憾，我们确实不受人类法律的保护。
t_en: Unfortunately, we are indeed not protected by human law.

t_ch: 啧，到头来便宜了你们。
t_en: Tch, so the IPC is still the final winner of it all.

t_ch: 可别这么说，这对匹诺康尼的股东来说可是一桩好事啊。
t_en: Don't say it like that. This is a good thing for Penacony's shareholders.

t_ch: 哼，匹诺康尼这块铜臭之地的规矩管不了我。Lancer，我们去收拾收拾那个老可爱。
t_en: Hmph. These Penaconian rules stinkin' of money won't keep me down. Lancer, let's go take care of that old fudger.

t_ch: 御主，虽然我双手双脚赞成你的观点，但时间似乎已经不够了。圣杯解体后，我们也该回到原来的地方了。
t_en: Master, while I'm all in on that suggestion, time's running out. Without the Holy Grail holding us up, us Servants need to go back to where we belong.

t_ch: 你们要走了吗？
t_en: Are you leaving already?

t_ch: 你谁来着？
t_en: Who are you again?

t_ch: 有些遗憾啊，哥们儿…如果还有机会见面，我一定请你去银河里最好的酒吧来两杯。
t_en: Such a shame, brother... If we ever cross paths again, I'll take you to the finest honky-tonk in the cosmos for a drink.

t_ch: 银河最好的酒吧吗？那可真令人期待。行啦，咱俩就不说什么告别的话了，别让这场难得的相聚染上悲伤的颜色。下次见，兄弟。
t_en: Finest in the cosmos, huh? That's something to look forward to. Alright, no need for a tearful farewell for us bros, let's not ruin the good run we've had together. Till we meet again, buddy.

t_ch: 还有你们两个，哎，真是的，每次都不能和你们痛痛快快地打上一场。
t_en: And you two... Seriously, why can I never get a proper fight with you?

t_ch: 是吗？明明我记得某人曾是我的手下败将来着？
t_en: Is that so? I distinctly remember beating you.

t_ch: 你知道的，我就是个普通的弓兵，就当这回我又输给你了吧。
t_en: You know me, just a humble archer. Let's call this one another loss on me.

t_ch: 切，懒得和你们争了——在消失之前，我想再去看看匹诺康尼的风景。
t_en: Tch, I'm done arguing with you lot. I'm gonna take another peek at Penacony's views before leaving for good.

t_ch: 那么也该是告别的时候了。御主，不得不说，虽然你的着装品味有待提升，但…作为从者，很高兴这一次圣杯战争能有你同行。
t_en: And so, it's time to say goodbye. Master, I have to admit that your fashion sense could use a little work, but... as a Servant, I'm genuinely glad to have fought by your side in this Holy Grail War.

t_ch: 夸夸其谈和华丽的装饰下，我能看见一个内心执着的孩子…偶尔做一回正义的伙伴，感觉还不错。
t_en: I can still see the determined heart of the child you once were underneath all your grand speeches and extravagant flair. You know, it would feel pretty good to be a hero of justice once in a while.

t_ch: 咳咳，告别的时候，精神分析什么的就免了吧。还有，论及衣品，咱们两个彼此彼此啊，「人类的守护者」！
t_en: Ahem, let's skip the last-minute psychoanalysis, alright? And when it comes to fashion... You're not much better than me, "Guardian of Humanity."

t_ch: 说来我也很好奇，作为「人类守护者」的我为什么会被召唤到这样一个没有抑止力的世界来——
t_en: That brings up a good point. Why would I, a "Guardian of Humanity," get summoned to a world without the Counter Force?

t_ch: Archer，你应该清楚自己被召唤来这里的原因。
t_en: Archer, you should know why you were summoned here.

t_ch: 你以英灵的身份被召唤。
t_en: You were summoned as a Heroic Spirit.

t_ch: 英雄啊…就当是无端做了一场美梦吧。
t_en: A hero, huh? ...I'll chalk it up as a beautiful dream.

t_ch: 男人冰冷的面孔上露出一丝浅笑，消失在众人眼前。很快，这里只剩下了最后的一对主从。
t_en: A faint smile tugged at the man's cold face, before he vanished from sight. Soon, only the final pair of Master and Servant remained.

t_ch: …很高兴能应你的召唤，来到这个奇妙的世界游历一番。
t_en: ...I'm glad I was summoned to this strange world and got to experience it with you.

t_ch: 我不擅长说再见…
t_en: I'm not great at farewells...

t_ch: 还有什么想叮嘱我的话吗？
t_en: Anything else you want to say to me?

t_ch: 在你的梦境里，我看到了你的畏惧…但，御主，请不要在意自己身为人类应有的那些情感：对未知的惶惑，对失去的恐惧，还有对自己做不到某事的悔恨。
t_en: I saw your fears in your dream... but Master, don't be burdened by the emotions that come with being human: the fear of the unknown, the dread of loss, or the regret of what you couldn't achieve.

t_ch: 在成为英雄前，赫拉克勒斯也曾是个孩子。
t_en: Even Heracles was just a child before becoming a hero.

t_ch: 即使现在的你还不够强大，我相信总有一天，你会成为照亮银河的星辰。
t_en: You may not be strong enough now, but I have faith that you'll eventually become a star that illuminates the entire cosmos.

t_ch: 就像你那样吗？
t_en: Like you did?

t_ch: 如果我的存在能偶尔为你照亮前路…那真是再好不过了。
t_en: If my existence can occasionally light the way forward for you... that would be more than I could hope for.

t_ch: 赫拉克勒斯是谁…
t_en: Who's Heracles...

t_ch: 御主，替我向未来的你问好。
t_en: Master, please send my regards to the person you will become in the future.

t_ch: 流光散去，英武的少女骑士随之消失，一如她的降临。
t_en: As the Luminflux dimmed, the valiant girl knight faded, vanishing just as she had arrived.

t_ch: 此时此刻，在流梦礁，圣杯战争的举办者正饶有兴致地欣赏风景……
t_en: At the same time, in the Dreamflux Reef, the host of the Holy Grail War is sitting elsewhere watching the scenery with interest...

t_ch: 圣杯…碎了啊，可惜。
t_en: The Holy Grail... broke. What a shame.

t_ch: 人们追逐它，为它战斗，为它奉献，为它生死，就像是…我们的匹诺康尼。
t_en: People have pursued it, fought for it, made sacrifices for it, and died for it, much like they did for our own Penacony.

t_ch: 他们将自己的梦注入其中，让匹诺康尼成为了如今的模样。那么，匹诺康尼有没有回应他们的愿望呢？
t_en: They poured their dreams into Penacony to shape the planet into what it is today. But the question is... has Penacony ever answered their dreams?

t_ch: 看看这个忆质黑洞吧，十个琥珀纪过去了，曾经坐在这里，从零开始建造匹诺康尼的人们，如今就只剩下一个卑鄙无耻的老头。他的朋友也好，他的敌人也罢，全都不在了。
t_en: Look at this Memoria Singularity. Ten Amber Eras have passed, and out of those who once came here to build Penacony from the ground up, only one despicable old man is left. His friends, his enemies... they're all gone.

t_ch: 我必须向两位坦白，我的愿望实现了：无论你们如何看我，无论我们曾如何憎恨对方，圣杯承认了各位的功绩，也让你们以英灵的面貌现身……
t_en: I must confess to both of you, my wish has been granted. No matter how you see me, no matter how deeply we hated each other in the past, the Holy Grail has recognized your achievements and brought you forth as Heroic Spirits...

t_ch: 两位难道不觉得，这也是一种别开生面的故友重逢吗？可惜，米哈伊尔这老小子却没能在场。
t_en: Don't you think this is a rather unique reunion of old friends? It's unfortunate that Mikhail, that old rascal, couldn't be here to join us.

t_ch: 奥帝先生，您的愿望真是让我感动。
t_en: Mr. Oti, your wish truly moves me.

t_ch: 要不是我认识你，就要被你这鬼话唬住了。故友重逢？不过是你生意的一些添头罢了。
t_en: If I didn't know you better, I'd almost be swayed by your sweet words. A reunion of old friends? No. It's just another part of your business deal.

t_ch: 葛瑞迪…呃，不，也许此刻我该称呼你为芮克先生才对？作品剪辑完成了吗？…这么重要的工作，还得看你啊。
t_en: Grady, or perhaps I should now address you as Mr. Reca, is the editing of the footage complete? ...That is such an important task, after all.

t_ch: 我剪去了涉及参赛者隐私的内容，保留了这场「圣杯战争」最精彩的部分。也许它在艺术性上谈不上是什么优秀的作品…但在商业性上，它应当不会令您失望的。
t_en: I've cut out everything related to the participants' privacy and kept only the most thrilling parts of the Holy Grail War. It may not be an artistic masterpiece, but it should meet your commercial expectations.

t_ch: 那就够了，你们这些艺术家，总想着在自己信奉的艺术丰碑上不惜一切代价凿下自己的名字…嚯嗬嗬嗬，可惜啊，如愿以偿者就像圣杯战争的胜利者一样少之又少吧？
t_en: That'd be enough for me. You artists always aim to carve your names into art's annals no matter the cost... Too bad those who actually succeed are as rare as the winners of the Holy Grail War.

t_ch: 我就不一样了，除开金钱回报，世上还有什么能更好地度量你为一件事所做的努力呢？
t_en: I'm not like that. What else can truly measure the effort you've put in apart from cold hard cash?

t_ch: …属于往昔「英灵」的记忆，也是一桩令我满意的报酬。
t_en: ...The memories of former Heroic Spirits are also worthy compensation.

t_ch: 喔？葛瑞迪那样的烂片导演的记忆？你倒是说说，那东西有什么价值？
t_en: Memories of a flop director like Grady? Do enlighten me, what worth do those hold?

t_ch: 若论电影人的记忆…其价值确实贱如尘埃。但若论人的记忆，那真是…一件无与伦比的珍物。
t_en: His memories are indeed positively worthless as a filmmaker, but when it comes to him as a person... those memories are treasures beyond compare.

t_ch: 挣扎、失意、抗争…最终又沦为无名的过客，这位前辈是那个匹诺康尼时代的缩影，是令人捧腹绝倒的小丑，也是货真价实的英雄。
t_en: He has had his struggles, disappointments, and defiance... only to be reduced to another nameless nobody. That man is a symbol of the Penacony of his times, a most ridiculous clown... And a most genuine hero.

t_ch: 这样如宝石般有着众多切面的记忆，我岂能错失？
t_en: How could I ever miss out on a memory with as many facets as a veritable diamond?

t_ch: 不过，我更好奇的倒是您，所费不赀，却铺陈出这么大一出闹剧，就不怕像葛瑞迪那般落得个不体面的下场？
t_en: But now I'm more curious about you, sir. You've spent so much money, only to create such an unsightly mess. Aren't you worried you'll end up in disgrace like Grady?

t_ch: 一个商人，如果活到我这个岁数还在乎体面，那他也太失败了。
t_en: A merchant at my age who still cares about dignity would be an absolute failure.

t_ch: 那么，这笔生意两清了，容我告退。
t_en: In that case, my business here is settled. I'll take my leave.

t_ch: 好了，该你了，阿斯娜小姐。我会签署声明，放弃你的名字注册而成的商标，还有对你一切创作成果的所有权的…这样，你满意了吗？
t_en: Alright. It's your turn, Miss Asna. I'll sign the declaration to renounce the trademark on your name and all rights to your creative works... Are you happy with that?

t_ch: 谈不上什么满意。但如果你敢说个不字，我会在消失前用尽全力掐住你那短到看不见的脖子，看着你咽气…虽然我明白，这样做根本杀不死你。
t_en: Happy? Never. But if you don't carry it through, I'll squeeze your puny neck with every ounce of my strength until I disappear... I'll watch you take your last breath, even if I know it won't actually kill you!

t_ch: 再见了，匹诺康尼最成功的商人。我有句真心话一直想对你说——
t_en: Goodbye, Penacony's most successful merchant. There's something I've always wanted to say to you —

t_ch: 早点去死吧！
t_en: I wish you were already dead.

t_ch: 嚯嗬嗬嗬！真是感人的告白哟！阿斯娜，希望你的愿望能有实现的那天。
t_en: Hahaha! Now that's a heartfelt confession! Asna, I do hope your wish comes true one day.

t_ch: 与此同时，在列车上
t_en: At the same time, aboard the Express.

t_ch: 在远离「战场」的一角，以Rider职阶被召唤的从者，似乎从一开始就没有加入战斗的想法。
t_en: Far from the heat of battle, a Servant summoned under the Rider class stays on the sidelines. From the very beginning, it seems he had no intention of fighting.

t_ch: 作为Rider，他曾亲手创作了一条永不沉没的小船，名唤「罗盘号」。作为Rider，他曾是个搭乘列车遨游星间的无名客。
t_en: As a Rider, he once built an unsinkable little ship named The Compass. As a Rider, he was once a Nameless who rode the stars aboard an interstellar Express.

t_ch: 时隔数百年的时光，少年以英灵之身终于回到了他曾辛勤工作过的地方。在这儿，有位老朋友等着他。
t_en: Centuries have passed. Now, as a Heroic Spirit, the young man finally returns to the place where he once worked tirelessly. An old friend awaits him here.

t_ch: …三月七乘客总爱带些稀奇古怪的东西回来，有次甚至还拿了把叫做「定分枪」的奇物，结果只给帕姆打了一分！
t_en: ...March 7th is always bringing back bizarre and curious things. One time, she even brought back something called a "Rating Pistol," and it only gave Pom-Pom a single point!

t_ch: 还有，要小心姬子乘客的咖啡，那是帕姆见过最可怕的饮料——但这事绝对不要当面告诉她。
t_en: Also, watch out for Himeko's coffee. It's the most terrifying beverage Pom-Pom's ever encountered — but please don't ever tell her that to her face.

t_ch: 比格兰霍姆家乡的茶还难喝？
t_en: Is it worse than the tea from Granholm's hometown?

t_ch: 难喝一百倍帕！
t_en: A hundred times worse!

t_ch: 噗，哈哈哈。真想品尝一口啊！
t_en: Pfft, hahaha. I'd love to have a try!

t_ch: 还有还有，{NICKNAME}总是拉着三月七乘客一起胡闹，现在，就连帕姆最看好的丹恒乘客，都快被带坏了。
t_en: And! And! {NICKNAME} is always dragging March 7th into trouble. Pom-Pom had such high hopes for Dan Heng, but even he's started to follow along now.

t_ch: 嗯嗯。
t_en: Mm-hmm.

t_ch: 哎，你们这些乘客，简直都一个样——米哈伊尔乘客，你又在发呆了，你在想什么？
t_en: You passengers are all the same — Mikhail, you're spacing out again. What's going through that head of yours?

t_ch: 列车长，看来，又到了该告别的时刻了。
t_en: Conductor, looks like it's time to say our goodbyes again.

t_ch: 你…还会回列车来吗？
t_en: Will you... be returning to the Express?

t_ch: 大概吧。听帕姆说起这些年来，许多像我一样的人登上列车，真是…太好了。
t_en: Maybe... I'm just happy to hear that so many people like me have boarded the Express over the years.

t_ch: 无名客们来来去去，有些人找到了他们的归宿，而有些人继续前行，就像银轨永远向前…这就是星穹列车。我相信，列车长的未来是不会寂寞的。
t_en: The Nameless come and go. Some find a place they want to stay while others keep moving forward, just like the silver rails that never stop... That's the Astral Express. I'm sure the conductor's future will never be lonely.

t_ch: 「圣杯战争」要结束了，漂泊的灵魂也该回到休憩中了。米沙…要回匹诺康尼了。
t_en: The Holy Grail War is coming to an end, and wandering souls must return to their rest. I... need to go back to Penacony.

t_ch: 米哈伊尔乘客，要记得喔，无论你们是否离开，星穹列车是无名客永远的家。帕姆…会一直留在这儿，欢迎你们回来！
t_en: Mikhail, remember: whether you leave or stay, the Astral Express will always be the home of the Nameless. Pom-Pom will always be here... waiting for your return!

t_ch: 那就约好了，列车长。下一次停靠匹诺康尼时，请为我、为拉扎莉娜、为铁尔南…请为我们鸣笛。我们要是能听见，一定会回来见您。
t_en: It's a deal then, Conductor. The next time the Express stops at Penacony... please sound the horn for me, Razalina, and Tiernan. If we can hear it... then we'll definitely come visit you.

t_ch: 那就，一言为定帕！
t_en: It's a promise!

t_ch: 把思绪收回到现在，如果能有一个实现愿望的机会，{NICKNAME}，你想实现什么愿望？
t_en: Gather your thoughts to the present. If there really is a chance to fulfill a wish, what would yours be, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 在心中默念愿望。
t_en: Make a silent wish.

t_ch: 毕竟，愿望这种东西，如果说出来就不灵了。
t_en: You see, wishes lose their power once spoken aloud.

t_ch: 还真有你的性格。
t_en: That does sound like something you'd say.

t_ch: 总之，老奥帝先生兴师动众的第一届「圣杯战争」也成了最后一届。
t_en: Anyway, the first Holy Grail War orchestrated by Old Oti ended up being the last one.

t_ch: 不过，由芮克先生和爱德华医生协助制作的梦泡电影，不仅在艾普瑟隆大卖特卖，甚至再度在群星间掀起了逐梦热潮。奥帝先生和匹诺康尼的公司分部借着这趟热潮大赚了一笔。
t_en: But the dream bubble movie co-produced by Mr. Reca and Dr. Edward became a huge hit. Not only did it fill up movie theaters in Epsilon, it even created a new dream-chasing trend throughout the stars. Mr. Oti and the IPC's Penacony branch made quite the fortune from it.

t_ch: 他怎么都不亏啊。
t_en: That old fox didn't lose a thing.

t_ch: 这老东西太阴了。
t_en: He's far too shrewd.

t_ch: 那我的出场费呢？
t_en: What about my appearance fee?

t_ch: 关于将家主和股东们牵扯进来一事，正如砂金先生料到的，绝大部分的事情，他都以「娱乐节目」的名义搪塞了过去。
t_en: As Mr. Aventurine guessed, Oti simply brushed everything off as "entertainment" when questioned about placing Family heads and shareholders in danger.

t_ch: 天价的赔偿与官司自然是少不了的，但我怀疑，在圣杯启动前，他早就已经猜到会发生什么了。
t_en: While compensation and lawsuits are inevitable, I suspect he already knew exactly what was coming even before the Holy Grail was activated.

t_ch: 老奥帝先生说，他原本打算单独付给你一笔赔偿金，但即使是他，也觉得这太庸俗了。
t_en: Now, Old Oti did say he planned to give you some money, but even he thought that was too crude.

t_ch: 所以，他把这场「圣杯战争」中最有价值的东西送给了我们。
t_en: So instead, he gave us the most valuable thing from this Holy Grail War.

t_ch: 庸俗？还是庸俗点好啊！
t_en: Too crude? No no no. Crude's good!

t_ch: 敢情这玩意儿是赔礼？
t_en: So THIS is his apology?

t_ch: 一鱼多吃的老东西。
t_en: That old fox, always managing to get the most out of everything.

t_ch: 嗯？圣杯里有什么东西——致{NICKNAME}，这张纸条好像是给你的。
t_en: Hmm? There's something inside the Holy Grail — I think this is a note for you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: {F#姐们}{M#兄弟}，看你们都挺忙的，我就带着虎克先走了。还得感谢老奥帝先生啊，他愿意买下我搞到手的这个稀罕货，咱们这才能有钱来一趟说走就走的旅行。
t_en: Hey {M#brother.}{F#sister.} Since you all looked pretty busy, I've headed out with Hook. Big thanks to Mr. Oti for buying this rare trinket I got my hands on. That's how I could afford this spontaneous trip.

t_ch: 说起来，你难道不好奇，老桑博想对圣杯许下的愿望是什么吗？
t_en: By the way, aren't you curious what old Sampo's wish to the Holy Grail was?

t_ch: 不好奇。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 我猜，你的回答肯定是「不好奇」。嗐，{F#姐们}{M#兄弟}，你不好奇，观众们还期待着呢。
t_en: I'm thinking you'll probably say "no." Owww, come on, {M#bro!}{F#sis!} You might not be curious, but the audience is still on the edge of their seats!

t_ch: 非常好奇。
t_en: I'm indeed curious.

t_ch: 我就知道，{F#姐们}{M#兄弟}你还惦记着咱老桑博，不瞒你说，观众也都期待着呢。
t_en: I knew it, {M#bro}{F#sis}! You're still thinking about old Sampo. And, I won't lie, the audience is waiting for it too.

t_ch: 各位，节目已经完结了，但如果有什么能实现我的愿望——
t_en: Alright, folks! The show's over! But if there's anything still out there that can hear and fulfill my wish —

t_ch: 「我希望世上人人都幸福、快乐、自由，得偿所愿。」
t_en: "I wish for everyone in the world to be happy, joyful, free, and have their every desire fulfilled."

t_ch: 以令咒下令，荡除一切邪恶之物吧，Saber！
t_en: By the Command Spell, I order you, Saber! Destroy all that is evil!

t_ch: 击溃它，Saber！
t_en: Crush it, Saber!

t_ch: （还有工作没做完……）
t_en: (There's still work to be done...)

t_ch: 交谈之余，波提欧手枪也已上膛。在这之后，就该和幕后黑手决一死战了。
t_en: As they talked, Boothill had already loaded his gun. Next up: the final battle with the mastermind.

t_ch: 面对即将出现的敌人，两位从者已跃跃欲试。
t_en: With enemies on the way, the two Servants were more than ready to fight.

t_ch: 随着谐乐大典的余波平息，星际和平公司如愿入驻美梦之星，星穹列车的无名客也成为股东之一。
t_en: As the echoes of the Charmony Festival fade, the Interastral Peace Corporation successfully establishes itself on the planet of dreams, with the Nameless of the Astral Express becoming one of the planet's shareholders.

t_ch: 如今，这座银河著名的度假胜地中，一场好戏又将上演——
t_en: Now, a grand spectacle is about to unfold yet again in this holiday resort famed throughout the cosmos —

t_ch: 艾普瑟隆的影视巨星、橡木家系的话事人，知更鸟女士领衔主演！
t_en: Starring the superstar of Epsilon and the head of the Oak Family, the Lead Actor is... Miss Robin!

t_ch: 酷炫幽默的无名客，星穹列车的开拓者友情加盟！
t_en: Featuring a special appearance from the stylish and witty Nameless, the Trailblazer of the Astral Express!

t_ch: 风度翩翩的霸道总裁，战略投资部的石心十人之一，砂金总监强势客串！
t_en: With a powerful guest appearance from the charismatic and commanding director, Aventurine, one of the Ten Stonehearts of the Strategic Investment Department!

t_ch: 知名的导演芮克与友人联手打造！更有苜蓿草家主、匹诺康尼商业大亨老奥帝先生独家赞助！
t_en: Directed by the renowned Mr. Reca and his trusted companions! Lastly, exclusively sponsored by Old Oti, the head of the Alfalfa Family and a business tycoon of Penacony!

t_ch: 还有斯科特，他谁来着？擅长模仿动物的公司专员？总之，市场开拓部也干了！
t_en: And then there's Skott... Wait, who? That IPC Specialist who's good at mimicking animals? Well, anyway, the Marketing Development Department is in on this too!

t_ch: 《美梦与圣杯战争》，寰宇各大娱乐频道火热上映中！
t_en: Sweet Dreams and the Holy Grail War, now airing across entertainment channels throughout the universe!

t_ch: 啧，说好来这儿碰面…{NICKNAME}这家伙，跑哪儿去了？
t_en: Tsk, aren't we supposed to meet up here? {NICKNAME}... where did they go?

t_ch: …不对劲，难道说有人捷足先登，趁我们来之前把{F#她}{M#他}和从者都干掉了？
t_en: ...Something's off. Maybe someone got the jump on them and took {M#him}{F#her} and {M#his}{F#her} Servant out before we got here.

t_ch: 恕我直言，如果有谁能轻而易举地击败那位Saber，那站在这里的我们，都不会有还手之力。
t_en: With all due respect, if that hypothetical enemy could easily defeat your friend's Saber, then none of us here would be walking away alive.

t_ch: 莫非，我们这位老朋友对{RUBY_B#我}公司成员{RUBY_E#}的出现有了警惕心，打算躲在暗处观察我们的来意？
t_en: Or maybe our old friend is concerned about {RUBY_B#my}an IPC employee{RUBY_E#} showing up, so they're lurking in the shadows to assess us?

t_ch: 喔，你们曾经是敌人吗？
t_en: Oh? Were you enemies once?

t_ch: 曾经的敌人，未必不能是现在的朋友啊。我说的对吧，牛仔？
t_en: Past enemies can still become present-day allies. Am I right, cowboy?

t_ch: 且不谈公司的人，{F#她}{M#他}至少该相信我这个一同出生入死的老伙计吧！喂，{NICKNAME}，我可以担保现在一切安全，该出来谈谈合作的事了！
t_en: Even with the IPC, {F#s}he should at least trust me after we went through so much together. Hey, {NICKNAME}! Come on out! Let's chat about joining forces as a team! You ain't gonna get hurt!

t_ch: 别喊了，在见面之前我盘算过许多可能，不过没想到，现场的情况却是最离谱的那个……
t_en: They aren't coming. I went through a lot of possibilities in my head before we got here, but I never expected to face the most ridiculous of them all...

t_ch: 有小可爱诈骗到了巡海游侠的头上。
t_en: ...A shirtbag pulled off a scam on a Galaxy Ranger.

t_ch: 听好了！欠爱的小可爱们，不管你们是谁，打算做什么，你们已经被我包围了！立刻现身，别等投胎时再后悔自己的反应慢了半拍！
t_en: Listen up, forkin' shirtbags! Ain't no matter who you are and what you wanna do, I've got you surrounded! Pop out now, or don't say I didn't warn ya when you stub your toe on the way to the underworld!

t_ch: 听到了没？不投降，就得等着投胎。真不愧是被公司重金通缉的罪犯，一开口就匪气十足，把我吓得肝颤肺抖呐……
t_en: Did you hear that? He's gonna kill us if we don't give ourselves up. Oh, he's every inch a serious criminal with a huge bounty on his head. I'm positively shaking in my boots with how he talks...

t_ch: 等等！你身边这位…不是，「石心十人」的砂金先生吗？！为什么公司通缉的重犯却和公司高管走在一块儿？
t_en: Wait! Isn't that... Isn't that Mr. Aventurine of the Ten Stonehearts!? Why is a criminal wanted by the IPC walking around with an IPC executive?

t_ch: 您要是被这该死的牛仔绑架了，就冲我眨眨眼！难道说…啧，你们是老相识了啊。
t_en: Sir! If that blasted cowboy's got you kidnapped at gunpoint, just gimme a blink! Or... or maybe... you two are actually friends?

t_ch: 「公司高管吃里扒外，和银河通缉犯暗通款曲」——Assassin，你可一定要把这美妙的一幕拍下来。
t_en: "An IPC Executive Secretly in Cahoots With a Cosmic Fugitive" — Assassin, you've got to grab this awesome scene on film.

t_ch: 你的从者是…芮克先生？不，仔细一看，气质上始终有些差异……
t_en: Your Servant is... Mr. Reca? No... Now that I'm looking at him, there's something off...

t_ch: 喂，芮克边上那人穿着公司的制服…你认识吗？
t_en: Hey. You know him? The guy in the IPC uniform next to Reca?

t_ch: 认得我这张脸蛋的公司成员比茨冈尼亚沙漠里的沙子都多…我哪儿能费心记得他们每一个？
t_en: There are more IPC employees who know my face than there are grains of sand in Sigonia's desert. You think I can remember them all?

t_ch: 混蛋！别以为顶着「石心十人」的名头我就怕你！战略投资部的手再长，也伸不到咱们这边来！
t_en: Wretched brat! You think I'm scared of you just cos you're a Stoneheart? The Strategic Investment Department's reach may be long, but it will never reach my neck of the woods!

t_ch: 等到我赢下圣杯，所有人都要看我的脸色行事！我要让你们跪在地上向我求饶，然后学狗叫，学猪叫，不！学蛤蟆叫！
t_en: After I win the Holy Grail, I will make everyone do as I say! I'll make you all kneel before me and beg for mercy, then have you bark like dogs, oink like pigs... no! Croak like frogs!

t_ch: 真是浪费啊，竟然用圣杯让人学动物叫唤……
t_en: What a waste. Using the Holy Grail to make people do animal noises.

t_ch: 所以，老奥帝居然向贵部门的人也寄出了请柬吗？可我记得他们往匹诺康尼派驻的成员寥寥无几，莫非你就是……
t_en: So Old Oti even sent invitations to your department? But I thought you folks barely stationed anyone at Penacony. Unless... unless your name is...

t_ch: 噫！难道您听说过我的名字？
t_en: Huh!? H—Have you actually heard of me?

t_ch: 算了，我怎么可能记得无名小卒的名字。
t_en: Hmm. Never mind. There's no way I'd ever remember the name of some nobody.

t_ch: 呵，你们这群高高在上的干部，从来不把咱们这些底层员工当回事。一句话把我派去仙舟那样的蛮荒之地，又一句话调来匹诺康尼！我本来还以为这会是个美差……
t_en: Ha! You high and mighty executives have never treated us basic workers as people! You would send me to a primitive post like the Xianzhou with one word, and switch me over to Penacony with the next! I thought Penacony might be a juicy gig...

t_ch: 谁知道…天天低着头和一群皮皮西谈业务，我的颈椎病都快恶化了！
t_en: ...But all I've done every day is talk business to a bunch of Pepeshi! It's making my neck crank even worse!

t_ch: 不过这些都将成为过去！砂金？呵，就让我代替你，成为新的石心十人！
t_en: But hey, all of that will soon be a thing of the past. Aventurine? Heh, I'll replace you and become the new Stoneheart of that name!

t_ch: 用你的宝具为我带来胜利吧，Assassin！
t_en: Assassin! Use your Noble Phantasm to bring me victory!!!

t_ch: 终于可以见识见识这家伙的从者了吗？Archer，准备战斗！
t_en: Ho? Do I finally get to see this shrimp's Servant in action? Archer, get ready for a fight.

t_ch: 你这乐在其中的样子是怎么回事？
t_en: What's with the happy smile?

t_ch: 拍摄已经开始，请演员们各就各位。
t_en: The filming has begun, actors. Take your positions!

t_ch: 就让相机启动，胶卷旋转，回到那<size=36><color=#dbc291ff>「永不腐朽的默片恐怖秀！」</color></size>
t_en: Let the camera roll, let the film spin, and let us return to <size=36><color=#dbc291ff>"The Silent Horror Picture Show"!</color></size>

t_ch: 演出，ACTION！
t_en: Ready... ACTION!

t_ch: 等等，这不是什么灵体化，而是凭空从我们的眼前消失了。这位从者的气息遮断，比我想象的还要诡异。等等——
t_en: Wait, this isn't spirit form. He just vanished, right before our eyes. This Servant's Presence Concealment is more disturbing than I imagined. Wait—

t_ch: 你声音呢？
t_en: Where's your voice?

t_ch: 我声音呢？<size=36>他宝贝的，他宝贝的！他宝贝的！</size>
t_en: Where's my voice? <size=36>Forkin' hell, forkin' hell! Son of a fork!</size>

t_ch: 别费劲了，我试着解析了下这个结界。在封禁声音的同时，似乎还有更危险的东西正在向我们靠近。
t_en: It's no use. I've tried analyzing this Bounded Field. It doesn't just mute sound, something far more dangerous is closing in.

t_ch: 如果没有声音，你又怎么知道我刚刚说了什么？
t_en: If there's no sound, then how'd you know what I just said?

t_ch: 瞧，这里有一行台词。
t_en: Look, there are subtitles here.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，我一直想看你们露出这副表情，这副像傻瓜一样被我玩弄于股掌的表情！
t_en: Hahahahaha! I've always wanted to see you people like this! A bunch of idiots subjected to my every whim!

t_ch: 不公平！为什么你有声音！
t_en: That's not fair! Why do you get to have a voice?

t_ch: 那当然是因为——我是主角！你们这些臭跑龙套的待遇能和我比？我已经等不及听这些家伙在这场恐怖展映会里发出震耳欲聋的尖叫了——
t_en: And why have you been reduced to this, may you ask? Because now I am the protagonist! You extras will never be treated in the same way as me! I can't wait to hear what kind of petrified screams you'd be making in this horror show...

t_ch: 哦豁，抱歉，我忘了，在Assassin的宝具中，没人能听到自己的尖叫。
t_en: Oh. Wait. Oops, I forgot. No one can hear their own screams in Assassin's Noble Phantasm.

t_ch: 片刻过后，牛仔的朋友们也一无所知地来到此处
t_en: A moment later, the cowboy's friends arrived, equally clueless.

t_ch: 总感觉好安静啊，既没看到波提欧先生，也没看到邀请我们过来的敌人。
t_en: It's unusually quiet. No sign of Mr. Boothill or the enemy who lured us here.

t_ch: 这句话可不兴说。
t_en: You shouldn't have said that.

t_ch: 警惕黑色高级飞车。
t_en: Be on guard for any black luxury speedster.

t_ch: Saber也是加把劲骑士。
t_en: Saber's a knight who gives her all too.

t_ch: 我会尽己所能保护各位，请不要离我太远。不过敌暗我明，再谨慎也无法保证万无一失。
t_en: I'll protect everyone to the best of my ability. Please don't stray too far from me. But with the enemy in the shadows and us out in the open, even the greatest caution can't guarantee our safety.

t_ch: 如此空旷的环境，如果对方派出Archer在远处发动袭击，我们会陷入相当被动的境地。
t_en: In a wide-open area like this, if the enemy sends Archer to attack from range, we'll lose the initiative and be at a serious disadvantage.

t_ch: 以我对老奥帝的了解，他会雇佣最卑鄙的家伙充当自己的马前卒，召来最邪恶的从者替他扫清障碍。
t_en: From what I know of Old Oti, he's the type to hire the lowest scum as his pawn, summoning the most wicked Servant to eliminate anything in his way.

t_ch: 你们难道没有发现，从刚才开始，我们开口说话时的声音不见了吗？
t_en: Haven't you noticed? Since earlier, our voices vanish when we speak.

t_ch: *清嗓*。
t_en: *Clears throat*

t_ch: 确实是这样…是敌人所谓的「宝具」导致的吗？
t_en: That's true... Could it be the enemy's so-called Noble Phantasm?

t_ch: 比起这些，各位，我有个问题——这个世界的历史中，有可能诞生非灵长类的英灵吗？比如，广告牌，或者电视机什么的。
t_en: More importantly, everyone, I have a question. In this world's history, is it possible for a non-primate to become a Heroic Spirit? Say, a billboard? Or maybe a TV?

t_ch: 若是智械的话…倒也不是不可能。但广告牌和电视机……？
t_en: If it were an Intellitron... that's at least plausible. But a billboard? A TV...?

t_ch: 那么，前面这家伙是……？
t_en: Then what's that thing up ahead...?

t_ch: 一台毫无预兆出现的电视机，此刻正以它光滑的后脑勺背对众生。
t_en: A TV, appearing out of nowhere, turns its polished rear to the crowd.

t_ch: 嘎吱，嘎吱，嘎吱。
t_en: Creak, creak, creak.

t_ch: 怎么看都是个陷阱吧……
t_en: It's obviously a trap...

t_ch: 先观望观望。
t_en: Let's stay back and observe for now.

t_ch: 别靠近那东西。
t_en: Don't go near it.

t_ch: 看看周围有没有异常。
t_en: Scan the surroundings and look for anything unusual.

t_ch: 如果这是他们的障眼法，真正的敌人多半就藏在这附近等我们上钩，还是先远离这个是非之地吧。
t_en: If this is a decoy, the real enemy's probably lurking nearby, waiting for us to take the bait. We should steer clear of this trap.

t_ch: 一股冰冷的力量将你们强行拽回了原地，而后，耳畔传来熟悉的声音。
t_en: A frigid force drags you violently back to where you started. And then, a voice you know all too well murmurs by your ear.

t_ch: 踏进了斯科特大人的地盘还想走？没门儿！
t_en: You want to leave after entering Boss Skott's turf? In your dreams!

t_ch: 我就知道是你小子。
t_en: I knew it was you.

t_ch: 怎么有狗在叫？
t_en: Why does it sound like a dog barking?

t_ch: 斯科特？是那位擅长仿生口技表演的公司专员吗？
t_en: Skott? The IPC Specialist who does those bionic voice impressions?

t_ch: 天哪，斯科特也是发达了，居然连知更鸟这种大明星都知道你的名字。
t_en: Unbelievable. Skott's really made a name for himself, even the acclaimed Robin knows who he is.

t_ch: ……好啦，是我告诉她的，我偶尔也要跟朋友聊聊旅途中的奇人异事嘛！
t_en: ...Alright, I'm the one who told her. Can't a {M#guy}{F#gal} share a few wild travel stories with {M#his}{F#her} friends every now and then?

t_ch: 横亘在你们之间的短暂沉默，足以表达这位老熟人所有的无奈和愤怒。
t_en: The brief silence that lingers between you was enough to convey every ounce of fury and exasperation from this old acquaintance.

t_ch: 瞧，又急。
t_en: See? Always so hasty.

t_ch: 破旧的电视机摇摇晃晃地发出噪音，但可惜，这是默片，你无法听到雪花噪点的沙沙声。
t_en: The battered TV wobbles, buzzing with static noise. But alas, this is a silent film. You can't hear a single crackle of the snowflakes.

t_ch: 你上电视了，哥！
t_en: You're on TV, bro!

t_ch: 都什么时候了，你还关心这个？
t_en: Seriously? It's not the time for this!

t_ch: 当心他的从者！那小子把这里改造成了——
t_en: Watch out for his Servant! That guy turned this whole place into—

t_ch: 没有配音的配角，就别说这么多台词浪费观众的时间了。
t_en: Supporting characters with no voice dubbing shouldn't waste the audience's time with your lines.

t_ch: 演出，ACTION！
t_en: Ready... ACTION!

t_ch: 静默三幕剧•其一
t_en: The Silent Stage: Act I

t_ch: 《午夜电锯狂想》
t_en: Midnight Chainsaw Rhapsody

t_ch: 领衔主演
t_en: Lead Actors

t_ch: 斯科特
t_en: Skott

t_ch: 你们在一个密闭的房间里醒来，沙沙作响的电视机是死亡的背景音乐。
t_en: You all wake in a sealed chamber, the TV's static crackle singing death's lullaby.

t_ch: 和各自从者失散的你们，偶然邂逅了另一对熟悉的敌人。至于你们的牛仔和歌星朋友，更是不见人影。
t_en: Separated from your Servants, you run into another pair of familiar enemies. As for your cowboy and songstress companions? Vanished without a trace.

t_ch: 你怎么在这儿？
t_en: Why are you here?

t_ch: 斯科特以你的名义，给波提欧先生发了封诈骗短信。然后就没有然后了。
t_en: Skott sent a scam text to Mr. Boothill in your name, and you know how that goes.

t_ch: 这孙子连招都不带变的。
t_en: That punk doesn't even bother switching up his tricks.

t_ch: 他不会暗恋我吧。
t_en: Don't tell me he's got a crush on me.

t_ch: 我想想这次让他学什么叫。
t_en: Let me think... how will he bark this time?

t_ch: 比起那种无名小卒，我更关心另一个问题——我们的从者被他弄到哪儿去了？在恐怖片里分头行动，可是大忌，先弄清现在的状况吧。
t_en: Forget that third-rate nobody, what I really want to know is: Where did he send our Servants? Splitting up in a horror movie? That's a death flag. Let's figure out what we're dealing with here.

t_ch: 我看房间里这怪物就挺扎眼的。
t_en: That monster in the room sure is an eyesore.

t_ch: 一具没有身份的尸体，先别管它为什么还能动。总之，你并未从它的身上找到线索，但我可以给你们一些小提示。
t_en: A nameless corpse lies before you, don't ask why it's still twitching. More importantly, you find no clues on it, but I've got a little hint for you all.

t_ch: 「你们是一群星际和平公司的员工，为了表彰你们本年度的杰出业绩，公司特地为你们组织了一场酒店度假。」
t_en: "You are employees of the Interastral Peace Corporation. To reward your outstanding performance this year, the IPC has organized a special vacation at a hotel."

t_ch: 「你们来到酒店，却发现这里除了自己以外空无一人。随着午夜降临，酒店也露出原本的模样。此地——」
t_en: "But when you arrive, the hotel is completely empty. As midnight strikes, its true nature is revealed. This place—"

t_ch: 已经是第五个房间了，<size=28>喂！能听到吗？</size>——啧，真是群不省事的家伙。
t_en: This is already the fifth room. <size=28>Hey! Can you hear me?</size> Tsk. What a bunch of troublesome fools.

t_ch: 竟然连Archer你也有被人骗到的时候。
t_en: I can't believe that even you fell for it, Archer.

t_ch: 事先声明，我是持反对意见的。耐不住有两个傻子要来探个究竟。
t_en: Just so we're clear, I was against this from the start. But those two blockheads insisted on investigating.

t_ch: 现在，是三个傻子了。异世界圣杯战争的御主们如此缺乏警惕心…真是令人眉头上的皱纹都变多了。
t_en: Now it's three blockheads. Masters of an otherworldly Holy Grail War, and not a hint of caution among them... That's how I end up with frown lines.

t_ch: 现在说这个是不是有点晚了？给我听好了，「此地——」
t_en: Isn't it a little late to say all that? Listen carefully, "This place—"

t_ch: 这场圣杯战争确实充斥着古怪。是我疏失了，未能尽从者的职责，让御主远离敌人的陷阱。
t_en: This Holy Grail War is strange indeed. It's my fault, I have failed as a Servant. I should've kept my Master away from this trap.

t_ch: 两位，我们在这儿！
t_en: You two, over here!

t_ch: 我被关进这个房间了！
t_en: I'm locked inside this room!

t_ch: 真是困了来枕头。
t_en: Right on cue.

t_ch: 御主？我看到你的字幕了，我这就想办法救你出来！
t_en: Master? I saw your subtitles! I'll find a way to get you out!

t_ch: 未必，如果我是敌人，就会伪装成御主，藏在门背后偷袭我们。
t_en: Hold on. If I were the enemy, I'd disguise myself as a Master and wait just behind that door for the perfect ambush.

t_ch: Archer，你听听我这声音，可是货真价实的吧？
t_en: Archer, listen to my voice! It's obviously me, right?

t_ch: 抱歉，这可是默片，在我的面前，只有门下面一串挂着「砂金」名字的字幕。想要证明自己，除非自曝十件只有我御主知道的糗事。
t_en: Sorry, this is a silent film. All I see is a line of subtitles under the door, with the name "Aventurine" slapped on it. If you want to prove you're really my Master, you'll have to spill ten humiliating secrets that only my real Master would know.

t_ch: 你这不完全清楚我是谁吗？
t_en: You really don't know who I am?

t_ch: 还有完没完了？！这是恐怖片，龙套们，给我保持恐惧！
t_en: Will this ever end!? This is a horror film, people! Background characters, start acting scared!

t_ch: 「此地本是公司流放犯人的监狱，布满了阻止出逃的致命机关。而监狱中的犯人早已消失殆尽，只剩一群前来索命的怨灵。」
t_en: "This place was once an IPC prison, where criminals were banished. Its deadly traps ensured none escaped. Now, the inmates have passed, leaving only bloodthirsty, vengeful spirits."

t_ch: 我从你讲第一句的时候就想问了，为什么要针对公司员工？
t_en: I've wanted to ask this since your first line. Why target the IPC Workers?

t_ch: 在这里还是监狱的年代，公司狗们将这里变成了人间地狱。而观众最爱看的，正是他们遭难的戏码。
t_en: Back when this was still a prison, the IPC scoundrels turned it into a living hell. What do audiences love most? Watching those criminals suffer.

t_ch: 只要锯掉任意一个人的腿，大门就会自动打开。但如果一个系统时后什么也没有发生——
t_en: Saw off someone's leg, and the door unlocks automatically. But, if nothing happens after a system hour—

t_ch: 这个房间就会原地爆炸！而身为住客们的你们也将在烈焰中粉身碎骨。
t_en: BOOM! The room explodes. You, the guests, will also go up in flames.

t_ch: 我知道这个！「不干某事就无法离开的房间」！这可是匹诺康尼古早恐怖片里最经典的元素！
t_en: I know this one! "The room you can't leave unless you do a particular thing!" It's one of the classic tropes from Penacony's vintage horror flicks!

t_ch: 是不是偏题了？
t_en: Aren't we a little off-topic?

t_ch: 你的阅片内容好狂野…
t_en: You watch rather wild films...

t_ch: 希佩在上！看不出来，你年纪轻轻竟然对匹诺康尼早期的梦泡电影有所了解，真是后生可畏。
t_en: By Xipe! I did not expect someone your age to know that much about Penacony's early dream bubble cinema. You have a bright future ahead.

t_ch: 没错，正是本人在匹诺康尼草创时期拍摄的第一部电影为后世留下了如此宝贵的天才创意。
t_en: That's right! I was the one who gave the world my genius vision with my first film in Penacony's early days!

t_ch: 这个所谓的「宝具」便是以此为原型么？这家伙开始得意忘形、胡言乱语起来了啊…干得好啊，御主。
t_en: So this "Noble Phantasm" is based on that? This guy's really getting carried away and started babbling... Nice work, Master.

t_ch: 难怪这段情节如此生搬硬套，那就可以理解了。将古老的恐怖片元素随意拼接在一起，实在是没什么新鲜感。
t_en: No wonder the scene feels so awkwardly shoved in. Now it all makes sense. Randomly cobbling together old-school horror tropes? Yeah, not exactly groundbreaking.

t_ch: 比如锯腿这个梗，原本是主角被磁流体脚链锁住了，必须锯掉自己的腿。但放在眼下，就纯粹是照猫画虎了。
t_en: Take that "sawing off someone's leg" bit. Originally, the protagonist was restrained by ferrofluid shackles, and had no choice but to cut their own leg off. But here? It's just a lazy copy-paste.

t_ch: 年轻人，我收回刚才对你的赞美，我会把你的尖叫放在<color=#dbc291ff>葛瑞迪影业</color>最显眼的位置循环播放。
t_en: Kid, I take back every compliment I said about you. Your screams will be played on loop in the most conspicuous spot at <color=#dbc291ff>Grady Films</color>.

t_ch: 主动真名解放，难道这也是你的戏剧效果的一环？
t_en: Revealing your True Name on purpose. Let me guess, just part of your dramatic flair, huh?

t_ch: Assassin你这个白痴！怎么把真名给暴露了？说好了真名对英灵来说意味着弱点呢？
t_en: Y—You fool! How did you let your True Name slip like that? Isn't that supposed to be a Heroic Spirit's greatest weakness? Assassin!?

t_ch: 呃…不必惊慌，制片人。我不是什么了不得的传奇英雄，你大可问问他们，葛瑞迪这个名字在匹诺康尼意味着什么？
t_en: Uh... don't panic, Producer. I'm not some legendary hero or anything like that. Just ask them. What does "Grady" even mean in Penacony?

t_ch: 你谁啊？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 我只知道克劳克影业…
t_en: I've only heard of Clock Studios...

t_ch: …还有希米亚影业。
t_en: ...And Himemia Films.

t_ch: 不出所料的回答。但你们很快就会知道，谁才是匹诺康尼最伟大的电影导演，而现在——请容我暂且退居幕后。
t_en: As expected. Soon, you'll see who Penacony's greatest director is. For now, I'll slip behind the scenes.

t_ch: 是要牺牲自己？还是在我的面前刀剑相向？你们大可利用最后的时间商量一番。不过，再纠结的话，炸弹可就要爆炸喽？
t_en: So, what will it be? Self-sacrifice? Or will you turn your blades at each other right before my eyes? Take what little time you have left and talk it over. But keep stalling, and well — tick, tock... BOOM.

t_ch: 哎，好不容易遇到个同好，就没点粉丝福利吗？
t_en: Oh, you finally meet a kindred spirit, and I don't get any fan benefits?

t_ch: 御主，你也听到了。选一个吧，是锯掉你的左腿呢？还是右腿呢？说实话，如果能买一送一，我比较想锯掉有令咒的那条胳膊。
t_en: Master, you heard him. Pick a leg, left or right? Honestly, if I could get a bonus, I'd rather go for the arm with the Command Spells.

t_ch: 喂！你好歹是我的从者，再怎么也考虑下锯掉{NICKNAME}的腿好不好？
t_en: Hey! You're my Servant, remember? At least consider cutting off {NICKNAME}'s leg instead!

t_ch: 等等，老朋友，别用这种眼神盯着我，我只是说着玩的。
t_en: Wait, old pal. Don't give me that look! I was kidding.

t_ch: 各位，不要进入敌人的节奏，被他牵着鼻子走。越是慌乱的时刻，越是要保持冷静。
t_en: Everyone, don't dance to his tune. Don't let him pull us along. The more chaos he sows, the calmer we have to be.

t_ch: 还不开始自相残杀吗？房间，要爆炸了喔？
t_en: Still not turning on each other yet? Room's about to blow!

t_ch: 只有锯掉我的腿了。
t_en: I'll have to saw off my leg.

t_ch: 锯掉砂金的腿。
t_en: Saw off Aventurine's leg.

t_ch: 你是什么职阶？Bomber？
t_en: What class are you? Bomber?

t_ch: 干脆我们投票决定吧，我投我那位花枝招展的御主一票。
t_en: Let's vote. I vote for my flamboyant Master.

t_ch: 哎，你就别落井下石了，真正的答案，你早就意识到了吧？
t_en: Hey, no need to egg things on. You already figured out the real answer, didn't you?

t_ch: 不能离开的房间和马上就要爆炸的炸弹——说得确实煞有介事，但是……
t_en: A sealed room. A bomb about to go off. It sounds dramatic, but...

t_ch: 以常情而论，作为从者把区区房门直接劈开不就好了？御主，请往后退两步。
t_en: Rationally speaking, as a Servant, wouldn't it be easier to just cut the door down? Master, please take two steps back.

t_ch: 持剑的少女怒喝一声，门锁「咔嚓」应声断裂，大门「滋扭」一声随之洞开。
t_en: The sword-wielding girl unleashes a fierce shout. Crack! The lock splits, and the door creaks open.

t_ch: 御主，请站到我身边来，我并不清楚那位Assassin要做什么…但这里依旧很危险。
t_en: Master, stay close to me. I don't know what that Assassin plans to do next... But it's still dangerous here.

t_ch: 竟然在恐怖片里悍然选择暴力对抗，异界的英灵真是毫无艺术素养！
t_en: Using brute force in a horror film? Heroic Spirits from another world clearly have no sense of art!

t_ch: 身为金发美少女，你难道不该主动承担起制造尖叫和等待英雄救美的角色？
t_en: A blonde beauty like you. Shouldn't you be screaming for help and waiting for the hero to save the day?

t_ch: 藏头露尾的Assassin，一旦让我走到你的面前，你想要发出多大的尖叫，让谁来救你都可以。
t_en: Skulking Assassin, once I reach you, scream all you want, call for anyone to save you.

t_ch: 那你得走到我的面前才行！由于你们的鲁莽，「酒店的电锯恶灵已经被响声唤醒了」。主角，轮到你登场了！
t_en: Then you'd better reach me first! Thanks to your recklessness, "the hotel's chainsaw spirit has been awakened by the noise." Protagonist, take the stage!

t_ch: 桀桀桀桀桀，想跑，有问过主角我的意见吗？
t_en: Kekekeke... Trying to run? Did I, the protagonist, ever gave you permission to run!?

t_ch: 你是贴我身上的狗皮膏药吗？
t_en: What are you? A piece of gum stuck to my shoe?

t_ch: 作为这部恐怖片的主演，当然要亲手，把你们一个一个撕碎！
t_en: As the star of this film, I'll be the one to personally tear you all apart, limb by limb!

t_ch: 这…哪有恶灵在恐怖片被受害者打得满地打滚的…算了，制片人，你先退场吧，之后还有用得着你的时候。
t_en: This... What kind of evil spirit gets beaten to a pulp by its victims? ...Ugh, forget it. Producer, take five. We'll be needing you again later.

t_ch: 等等——
t_en: Wait—!

t_ch: 这就是异界英灵的实力吗…哼，也是，匹诺康尼不过是只有十个琥珀纪的弹丸之地，论创造传奇和历史的积累，自然比不上真正的英雄。
t_en: So this is the power of Heroic Spirits from another world? ...Hmph. Figures. Penacony's just a backwater speck with ten Amber Eras. With no real legend or history, of course we pale in comparison to true heroes.

t_ch: 但这恰恰会成为你们失败的理由。我会把你们这些逃亡者一个个地收下，变成听话的群演。
t_en: But that's also why you're going to lose. I'll take each of you runaways, one by one, and turn you into obedient extras for my film.

t_ch: 你到底哪只眼睛看到我们在逃跑？
t_en: Pray tell, does anyone look like they're running from you?

t_ch: 请稍安勿躁，电影的第二幕即将开始。
t_en: Don't get ahead of yourselves. Act Two is about to begin.

t_ch: 在短暂的休憩后，你们互相交流了情报。
t_en: After a brief pause, you exchange the information you've gathered with one another.

t_ch: 波提欧、知更鸟和音符小姐下落不明，而斯科特和他的从者随时准备卷土重来。
t_en: Boothill, Robin, and Miss Note are all still missing. Skott and his Servant could strike again at any time.

t_ch: 竟然还留给我们喘息的时间，看来，敌人对自己的大本营很自信嘛。
t_en: So they're giving us time to breathe, huh? Guess they're pretty confident about their trump card.

t_ch: Archer的御主，我看你对电影很熟悉。关于那个<color=#dbc291ff>葛瑞迪</color>，你有什么头绪吗？
t_en: Archer's Master, you seem very familiar with films. What do you know about this <color=#dbc291ff>Grady</color>?

t_ch: 我只是听说过这个名字，这家伙是个活跃在匹诺康尼早期时代的烂片导演，拍摄过数量惊人、低成本的恐怖默片，特别擅于玩弄一些有趣的商业噱头。
t_en: I've only heard the name before. The guy was some terrible director back in Penacony's early days. He cranked out a ton of cheap horror silent films and was a master of interesting commercial gimmicks.

t_ch: 不过他的名字早就被匹诺康尼人遗忘了。他用来关住我们的这部电影，是我偶然在星际旧货市场淘到的。
t_en: But people in Penacony forgot about his name ages ago. I only found the film he used to trap us through sheer luck — picked it up at an interstellar junk market.

t_ch: 竟然是个没什么名气的家伙吗？这可麻烦了。
t_en: So... we're dealing with a total nobody? That's not good.

t_ch: 这话怎么讲？
t_en: What makes you say that?

t_ch: 作为留名历史的存在，英灵的传说中往往潜藏着他们的弱点：像是某位战士沐浴冥河水刀枪不入，但偏偏伤害他的脚踵却会一击致命。
t_en: As Heroic Spirits who left a mark in history, our legends usually hint at our greatest weaknesses — like that one warrior who bathed in the river of the underworld and became invincible except for his heel.

t_ch: 我本以为能从你们口中打听到关于这家伙过去的事迹。没想到竟然是个你们也闻所未闻的无名小卒。
t_en: I was hoping you'd know something about that person's past. But turns out he's just some washed-up no-name that even you folks have not heard about.

t_ch: 倒是和斯科特先生很配啊，无名小卒，手段还挺阴险。
t_en: Fits right in with Mr. Skott, doesn't it? A pair of nobodies with shady tricks up their sleeves.

t_ch: 不过我有种直觉，就算他们此刻占据上风，也对我们无可奈何。
t_en: Still, I've got this feeling... even if they've got the upper hand now, they've got no real way to finish us.

t_ch: 咱们可都还好好的呢。
t_en: We're all just fine for the time being.

t_ch: 耍弄诡计的人没什么力量…
t_en: Tricksters never pack much of a punch anyway...

t_ch: 没错。生前是电影导演，显然成为英灵后也不会是什么擅长战斗的类型……
t_en: Right. He was a film director in life. That means he's not gonna suddenly become a powerhouse after turning into a Heroic Spirit.

t_ch: 这一点从他的宝具就能看出来，以生前曾拍摄过的恐怖片为原型，化作固有结界。虽然能力很稀有，但更像是个陷阱，只能困住受害者。
t_en: His Noble Phantasm proves it. He built it off one of his old horror flicks — basically a Reality Marble. Though it's a rare ability, it feels more like a trap, one that only ensnares its victims.

t_ch: …但陷阱中的尖刺要是伤不了猎物，那倒霉的就该是猎手了。
t_en: ...But if the trap can't hurt the prey, then it's the hunter who'll be in trouble.

t_ch: 现在，这家伙肚子里装了三位从者，而最坚固的城堡，往往都是从内部被打破的。
t_en: Now he's got three Servants in his stomach, while the strongest fortresses always fall from the inside.

t_ch: …不愧是真正的领袖，总能准确地判断局势。
t_en: ...You really do have the mind of a leader, always so sharp on reading the situation.

t_ch: 我也得再加把劲了，虽然这家伙没什么明确的弱点，但如果能<color=#dbc291ff>找到Assassin的本体</color>，用弓箭好好地给他理个发。他创造的固有结界也就不复存在了吧。
t_en: I'd better pull my weight too. He might not have an obvious weak spot, but if we can <color=#dbc291ff>find Assassin's true body</color> and let my arrows give him a proper haircut, that Reality Marble of his would no longer exist.

t_ch: *鼓掌*看来大家已经有了明确的目标了。
t_en: *clap hands* Sounds like everyone's got their sights set.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，虽然过去我曾和列车诸位交过手，但这都是各为其志——这一回，我对圣杯并没有什么兴趣。我的上司对老奥帝先生打算做什么更好奇。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I know I've butted heads with the Express Crew before, but that was just a difference in perspective. As for this time, I've got no interest in the Holy Grail. My boss is more concerned about what Old Oti has up his sleeve.

t_ch: 如果可以，希望这一次咱们能有好好合作的机会？
t_en: If you're willing... maybe we can actually work together this time?

t_ch: 成交。
t_en: Deal.

t_ch: 看你表现。
t_en: It all comes down to how you perform.

t_ch: 你瞧，御主，知更鸟小姐想要建立的同盟，已经慢慢有雏形了。军队有了目标，接下来就该行动了。
t_en: You see that, Master? Looks like Miss Robin's alliance is starting to come together. This army has a goal, and now it's time to move.

t_ch: 我们来的时候看到出口就在那边，趁现在离开这里吧。
t_en: We saw the exit on the way in. Let's head that way while we still can.

t_ch: 似乎我们几个又回到了原地……
t_en: Feels like we've just looped back to where we started...

t_ch: 如果没什么对应的法子，恐怕转到地老天荒也出不去。我说，御主，你要不要试试看把手上的筹码和骰子之类的东西留下来当路标？
t_en: If we don't find some kind of countermeasure, we could be stuck running in circles forever. Master, why not leave some of your chips and dice behind as markers?

t_ch: 不用你说，刚才我已经试过了…只是没想到，葛瑞迪还挺贪财。
t_en: You don't need to remind me of that. I've already tried it... But didn't expect Grady to be such a greedy money-grubber.

t_ch: 这位龙套先生，可不要污人清白！我只是按惯例打扫片场，免得无关紧要的道具出现破坏了场景。
t_en: Hey, hey! Don't go throwing shade, extra boy! I was just cleaning up the set as usual. Gotta make sure random props don't ruin the scene.

t_ch: 接下来这场戏，讲的就是公司员工被困在怨灵的回廊里，受永恒折磨的故事。
t_en: The next scene is all about IPC employees trapped in a haunted hallway and cursed to suffer forever.

t_ch: 没有出逃的方向，没有谁会伸出援手，在此，你们的任何挣扎都是无意义的。
t_en: Nowhere to escape, and no one will ever come to help. In here, any struggle is just wasted effort.

t_ch: 这是…知更鸟小姐的歌声吗？
t_en: Is that... Miss Robin's singing?

t_ch: 葛瑞迪，看来你已经忘了，被你困在宝具里的可不只有我们几个。
t_en: Grady, looks like you forgot that you didn't only trap US in your Noble Phantasm.

t_ch: 这就是经费不足的悲哀，葛瑞迪，在你因自编自导自演而应接不暇的时候，片场的另一边，知更鸟小姐已经找到打破僵局的办法了。
t_en: That's what you get for cutting the budget, Grady. While you're busy writing, directing, and starring as yourself, Miss Robin already found the way out on the other side of the set.

t_ch: 各位，跟随音乐前进吧！
t_en: Everyone, follow the music!

t_ch: 一股温和的同谐之力飘荡在狭窄的空间内，在它的中心，两位歌者正在为迷失者指引方向。
t_en: A gentle force of Harmony floats through the tight space. At its heart, two singers light the way for the lost.

t_ch: 与此同时，邪恶的怨灵也找上了她们，誓要将这些天外来客撕碎，吞没！
t_en: But at the same time, evil spirits approach them, determined to shred and consume these visitors from beyond the sky!

t_ch: 小心，知更鸟小姐！
t_en: Miss Robin! Watch out!

t_ch: 你这欺软怕硬的混账！
t_en: You coward, pick on someone your own size!

t_ch: 我以令咒——
t_en: By my Command Spell —

t_ch: 不管你打算做什么，都已经来不及了。
t_en: Whatever you have in mind, it's already too late.

t_ch: 以肉眼难以捕捉的速度，音符小姐抓住袭击的怪物，残暴地将它们的脑袋砸向地板。
t_en: In a blur too fast to track, Miss Note grabs the attacking monsters and viciously smashes their head into the floor.

t_ch: 「哦！我的显像管！啊！别用话筒砸我屏幕！嗷！」哐哐哐，砰砰砰。
t_en: "Oh no! My cathode ray tubes! Ahh! Don't hit my screen with your microphone! Ow!" Clunk clunk clunk, bang bang bang.

t_ch: 客观来讲，这是你在这部电影里看到的第一场少儿不宜的内容，你一度怀疑，知更鸟召唤的其实是Berserker。
t_en: Honestly, that might be the first R-rated scene in this whole movie. You start to wonder if Robin actually summoned a Berserker.

t_ch: 就这？当年阿斯德纳监狱的看守们可比这些东西难对付多了。
t_en: That's it? The guards back in Asdana Prison were tougher than these things.

t_ch: Bravo！不愧是匹诺康尼最艳丽的花朵，无论文戏还是武戏都同样出色。
t_en: Bravo! Truly Penacony's brightest bloom — brilliant in both drama and action.

t_ch: 这拙劣的服化道，还有乱七八糟的剧情——想想除了你也没别人了，葛瑞迪。
t_en: This terrible wardrobe, the nonsense plot... No one else could've cooked this up but you, Grady.

t_ch: 我好不容易从坟墓里爬出来，别再用你那烂片来吵我的眼睛！
t_en: I spent so much effort clawing my way out of the grave, so quit burning my eyes with your bargain-bin flicks!

t_ch: 当然，把片场留给演员，才是一位导演的生存之道。
t_en: Of course... real directors know when to leave the stage to the actors.

t_ch: 你们没事…真好。
t_en: You're all okay... thank goodness.

t_ch: 免费听了场演唱会。
t_en: I watched a free concert.

t_ch: 音符小姐…好酷。
t_en: Miss Note... is so cool.

t_ch: 你和葛瑞迪他们好像很熟…
t_en: You and Grady seem to have a bit of history...

t_ch: 一段孽缘罢了。
t_en: Let's just say... it was unpleasant.

t_ch: 两位，我们又见面了。作为Caster，你的战斗方式…实在让人眼前一亮。
t_en: We meet again. As a Caster, your combat style... was quite a surprise.

t_ch: 怎么看，这也不能算一场魔术师的战斗…看来在匹诺康尼，人们对Caster的定义真是足够宽泛。
t_en: Can't say that counted as a fight between mages... Guess people in Penacony really have a broad definition of Caster.

t_ch: 是吗？我倒觉得挺正常的。
t_en: Really? I thought it was pretty normal.

t_ch: 用双刀战斗的你，可没资格做出评价。
t_en: An Archer dual-wielding swords isn't qualified to talk about this.

t_ch: 多亏音符小姐的保护，我才能成功用同谐的力量摸索出正确的方向。
t_en: I managed to feel out the right path using the power of Harmony, all thanks to Miss Note's protection.

t_ch: ███，方才看到您在战斗时的背影，即使您没法说出自己的名字，我也已经猜到了。您是个伟大的人，我可以如此断言。
t_en: ██████, I saw you covering me in that fight. Even if you can't say your name... I know you must be someone great. I believe in that wholeheartedly.

t_ch: 御主，这无法改变现状，只要没有真名，我依旧只是手无缚鸡之力的鸢尾花艺者。
t_en: Master, this cannot change the present situation. Without a True Name, I'm just a weak Iris Family artist.

t_ch: 不，你刚才舍身保护御主的姿态已经证明，你是一位相当优秀的从者。
t_en: No. The way you shielded your Master just now proves you're an exceptional Servant.

t_ch: 你们不会理解，当名字都不属于你自己，价值被压榨干净的绝望。
t_en: You'll never understand the despair when even your name is taken away, and you've been wrung dry of all worth.

t_ch: 没错，一无所能，就连保护自己的御主都相当勉强，更别提赢下这场圣杯战争了。
t_en: Exactly. Powerless. Barely able to protect her Master, let alone win this Holy Grail War.

t_ch: 不如和我们合作，就像以前那样。███，奥帝先生会实现你所有的愿望。
t_en: Then why not work with me again... like before? ███, Mr. Oti can grant you all your wishes.

t_ch: 闭嘴！你这艺术涵养不如自动乐器的白痴！你的作品甚至不如一张报废的胶卷有艺术价值——
t_en: Shut up! You're a fool with less artistic taste than an automatic instrument! Your work doesn't even have the artistic value of a ruined roll of film!

t_ch: *重重叹气* …葛瑞迪，抱歉，我说得太过了。但你为什么要替{RUBY_B#老奥帝}那个落井下石的矮胖子{RUBY_E#}卖命？
t_en: *heavy sigh* ...Sorry, Grady. That was a bit harsh. But why are you serving that {RUBY_B#Old Oti}opportunistic fat old fart{RUBY_E#}?

t_ch: 躲藏在画外音中的葛瑞迪也叹了口气，声音无限伤感——
t_en: Hiding somewhere off-screen, Grady sighs too, his voice full of melancholy...

t_ch: 当然是因为，我受他召唤。唉，即便死去，被人遗忘，匹诺康尼也像个守财奴一般，贪婪地握紧我们的灵魂。想开些甜心，这一次至少有机会改写生前的失败。
t_en: Because he summoned me, of course. Even in death, forgotten by the world... Penacony clutches our souls like a miser. But hey, sweetheart — at least we have a chance to rewrite our past failures this time.

t_ch: 气氛太沉闷了，这可不行！让我们迎接《午夜电锯狂想》的最后一幕吧！在充斥着怨灵的酒店尽头，真正的邪恶等待着你们。
t_en: This gloom won't do! Let's kick off the final act of Midnight Chainsaw Rhapsody! The true evil awaits at the end of this haunted hotel!

t_ch: 哇哦，这回导演可真是下血本了。
t_en: Whoa, the director really went all-in this time.

t_ch: 葛瑞迪，这就是你说的，「给我更强大的力量」？
t_en: Grady... is this what you meant by "giving me more power"?

t_ch: 幸存的主角终于来到酒店深处，恶灵借由屏幕现身。而我将灯光与特效全都聚拢在你的身上，你将被我「塑造」成无敌的怪物。
t_en: The surviving protagonist finally reaches the heart of the hotel. The evil spirit appears through the screen. Then, I gather all the lights and effects on you, "shaping" you into an invincible monster.

t_ch: 林登•斯科特，你会化身一支庞大的恶灵军团，成为影史留名的经典恐怖形象。
t_en: Lyndon Skott, you'll become a legion of evil spirits, a horror icon etched in movie history!

t_ch: 燃起来了！{NICKNAME}，我失去的一切，今天都要你千倍奉还！
t_en: I'm all FIRED UP! {NICKNAME}! Now... I will take back everything you took from me. You shall pay tenfold... No. A thousand fold!

t_ch: 说实在的，这家伙死咬着你不放的坚韧，就连不列颠最癫狂的猎犬也要自愧不如。御主，你到底对他做了什么？
t_en: Honestly, the way this guy keeps coming for you? Even the wildest British hounds would call it obsessive. Master, what did you do to him?

t_ch: 一些私人恩怨…
t_en: Just some private disagreements...

t_ch: 私人恩怨？我斯科特因为你从中作梗三番五次被迫学动物叫、被贬职调职的悲惨人生，竟然被你轻描淡写地概括成一句「私人恩怨」？
t_en: Private disagreements? You ruined my life! You made me bark and oink and face a demotion, moving from one puny post to another in the cosmos in despair and misery... And this is what you call it? "Private disagreements"!?

t_ch: 怪我年少轻狂…
t_en: Look, I was young and didn't know what I was doing...

t_ch: 年少轻狂？我斯科特因为你从中作梗三番五次被迫学动物叫、被贬职调职的悲惨人生，竟然被你轻描淡写地归咎于「你的年少轻狂」？
t_en: Young and didn't know what you were doing? You ruined my life! You made me bark and oink and face a demotion, moving from one puny post to another in the cosmos in despair and misery... And this is what you call it? "Young and didn't know what you were doing"!?

t_ch: 现在和解…还来得及吗？
t_en: Is it still possible... To make it up to you now?

t_ch: 和解？因为你从中作梗让我坠入了三番五次被迫学动物叫、被贬职调职的悲惨人生，而你竟敢厚颜无耻地说想要和解？
t_en: Make it up to me? You ruined my life! You made me bark and oink and face a demotion, moving from one puny post to another in the cosmos in despair and misery... And this is all you have to say? "Make it up to me"!?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}…还有你身边这群龙套，也就能猖狂这几秒了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}... and your little entourage of extras... you won't get to be cocky for much longer...

t_ch: 源源不断的力量正在我的身体里喷涌，分裂！一个斯科特、两个斯科特、三个斯科特…后脑勺好痒，有两个我卡住了。
t_en: The power inside me is breaking out, trying to be free! One Skott, two Skotts, three Skotts... Omph, my scalp is scratchy. Two got stuck in there.

t_ch: 御主，台词！念登场台词！
t_en: Master, your line! Say your entrance line!

t_ch: 畏惧我吧，凡人们！在影子下，在睡梦里，在你背后，我无处不在！现在，我，群狼的主人，千面的斯科特将撕咬万物！
t_en: Fear me, mortals! For I am everywhere! In your dreams, in your shadows, right behind your back! Now I, the lord of the wolfpack, the Thousand-Faced Skott, will devour all!

t_ch: 今日我们欢聚于此，是为了深切缅怀牺牲的众多斯科特，纪念他们反复献出自己的生命……
t_en: We are gathered here today to commemorate the many Skotts, who have sacrificed their lives again and again...

t_ch: 要不是他们这些累赘拖后腿，我早赢了！
t_en: If those dead weights weren't dragging me down, I'd have won ages ago!

t_ch: 真正的孤狼总是独行，只有乌合之众才成群结队。
t_en: The true lone wolf walks alone. Only weak rabble sticks together.

t_ch: 你这贱人全程都躲在后面，我的眼睫毛出的力都比你多。
t_en: Coward! You've been hiding at the back all this time. Even my eyelashes did more work than you.

t_ch: 斯科特们你一言我一语，相互推卸着责任，好不热闹。
t_en: The Skotts start bickering and tossing blame around. What a scene.

t_ch: 哎，和这样的家伙对战，真是毫无荣誉可言。
t_en: Ugh, fighting someone like this? There's no honor in it.

t_ch: 葛瑞迪！你还想不想赢下圣杯了？现场改写剧本，改变布景！给我更多的力量，让我碾压他们！像个真正的魔鬼那样，把他们捏在掌心！
t_en: Grady! Do you still even want the Holy Grail? Go change the script on the fly! Move the stage props! Give me more power... let me crush them all! Crush them in the palm of my hands like a true villain!

t_ch: 抱歉，斯科特先生，是我错看你了。
t_en: Sorry, Mr. Skott. I was wrong about you.

t_ch: 你是个天生的喜剧演员，只适合扮演丑角。让你混入恐怖电影，不仅是对你角色特质的浪费，更是对镜头的亵渎。
t_en: You're a natural comedian who's only good at playing comical characters. Dropping you into a horror flick? That's not just a miscast, but an insult to the lens.

t_ch: 英雄所见略同。
t_en: Great minds think alike.

t_ch: 还是说得保守了。
t_en: Actually, I think that was being generous.

t_ch: 不准骂我的开心果！
t_en: Hey! Don't insult my little ray of sunshine!

t_ch: 葛瑞迪！你还想造反不成？别忘了，我可是你的御主，只要我动用令咒，你就得——呱？呱呱呱呱呱？
t_en: Grady! Are you trying to betray me? Don't forget, I am your Master. With my Command Spell, you'll have to — ribbit? Ribbit, ribbit? Ribbit? Ribbit!

t_ch: 区区三道令咒，就想在我的片场里支配我？斯科特先生，你被解雇了。
t_en: Trying to dominate me in my set with just three Command Spells? Mr. Skott, consider yourself fired.

t_ch: 各位演员，让你们看到了导演与演员争执的不堪场面。小瞧了「同谐」的力量，是我的失误，我也不该让你们聚在一块。
t_en: Apologies, cast and crew — you had to witness that little meltdown between director and actor. Underestimating the power of Harmony was on me. I also shouldn't have brought you all together.

t_ch: 所以，我会尽心尽力地调整各位登场的时机。在下一场电影里，我将继续享用你们刺耳的尖叫。
t_en: So, I'll be more thoughtful with your stage entrances next time. I look forward to hearing your piercing screams all over again in our next film.

t_ch: 静默三幕剧•其二
t_en: The Silent Stage: Act II

t_ch: 《钟表惊魂夜》
t_en: A Haunting of Clocks

t_ch: 领衔主演
t_en: Lead Actors

t_ch: 我们…离开了他的固有结界？
t_en: Did we just... leave his Reality Marble?

t_ch: 不对劲。
t_en: Something's off.

t_ch: 「每当夜晚降临，钟表乐园便会露出不为人知的一面。」
t_en: "Each night, the Clockland will reveal its unknown side."

t_ch: 「身为主人公的你睁开眼，却发现自己正身处一场无法逃离的噩梦。现在，一位可怕的敌人已来到你的身旁。」
t_en: "As the protagonist, you open your eyes and find yourself trapped in a nightmare with no exit. And now... a terrifying enemy is standing right beside you."

t_ch: 明知我们是同盟关系，还要挑拨离间？
t_en: Knowing we're allies, you still go out of your way to stir up trouble and sow discord?

t_ch: Archer先生，你可不像是个会死板遵守盟约的从者。趁着我特地安排的「分头行动」，替御主解决一个对手，岂不美哉？
t_en: Mr. Archer, you don't strike me as someone married to a contract. Wouldn't it be smart to take this "split-up operation" and eliminate a rival for your Master?

t_ch: 这建议的确值得参考。
t_en: I might give it a thought.

t_ch: 不要被策反啊！
t_en: Don't let him get in your head!

t_ch: 呱，Saber救我。
t_en: *ribbit* Saber, help me!

t_ch: 打就打，谁怕谁。
t_en: Bring it on. I'm not scared.

t_ch: 「要杀掉{F#她}{M#他}么？」在你醒来前，我曾<unbreak>12</unbreak>次推翻这个问题的答案。如果我要出手，你不会有机会睁开眼睛。
t_en: "Should I kill {F#her}{M#him}?" I turned that question over twelve times before you woke up. If I had decided to act, you wouldn't even get the chance to open your eyes.

t_ch: 可惜，我本以为你是个聪明人。那你们可就要受苦了——
t_en: Too bad. I thought you were smarter than this. Guess you'll have to suffer for it.

t_ch: 「邪恶的钟表匠从地狱带来掌管不同诅咒的恶灵，它们伪装成乐园里人畜无害的玩偶，将受害者拉入睡梦，无休止地折磨他们的灵魂。」
t_en: "The wicked Watchmaker brought from hell evil spirits that wield different curses, disguised as harmless amusement park mascots. They drag victims into sleep and torture their souls for eternity."

t_ch: 我可是克劳克影业的导演，你有我们钟表小子的版权吗？
t_en: Excuse me! I'm the director at Clock Studios — do you own the rights to our Clockie?

t_ch: 别担心，奥帝先生的公关团队会替我解决的——上辈子我做梦都想把克劳克影视乐园改造成我的噩梦乐园，没想到，竟然以这种方式实现了。
t_en: Don't worry. Mr. Oti's PR team will handle it. I used to dream of turning Clock Studios Theme Park into my own nightmare park. Who knew I'd get my wish this way?

t_ch: 真是比吃了三层夹心的仰望星空派还畅快的体验。好好享受一番吧，亲爱的角色们，直到……
t_en: It's even more satisfying than biting into a triple-stuffed Stargazer's Pie. Soak it in, dear character, until...

t_ch: <size=36>成为恶灵的玩具！</size>
t_en: <size=36>YOU BECOME TOYS FOR THE EVIL SPIRITS!</size>

t_ch: 如果你打算愣在这里，我不介意考虑Assassin的建议，让你提前退场。
t_en: If you're just gonna stand there, I might take Assassin's advice and write you out early.

t_ch: 走吧，按那位大导演的说法，我们该去被恶灵折磨了。
t_en: Come on. According to that great director, it's time for us to be tortured by the evil spirits.

t_ch: 抱歉，亲爱的，差点忘了，你的声音已经是我的私产。
t_en: Oh, my bad, sweetheart. Almost forgot — your voice is already my personal property.

t_ch: 最开始是歌曲的版权，然后是形象，最后是名字……
t_en: First it was the copyright of your songs, then your image, and finally... your name.

t_ch: 他们找来一个比你更年轻漂亮的女孩，███这个名字成为她在舞台上的艺名。而你，在梦境的荒原里死不瞑目。
t_en: They found a younger, prettier girl, slapped the name "███" on her, and put her on stage. And you? You'll die in despair in the desert of Dreamscape.

t_ch: 我会慢慢吞掉你的身子，哪怕是音符小姐这个借来的假名，也会成为我的所有物。
t_en: I'll devour your body piece by piece, and even that fake name Miss Note will be mine.

t_ch: 这不是版权，是垄断！
t_en: That's not copyright. That's monopolization!

t_ch: 快放开音符小姐！
t_en: Let go of Miss Note!

t_ch: 妖孽，还不束手就擒！
t_en: Monster! Surrender, or face justice!

t_ch: 终于来了，正好让你的朋友们也欣赏欣赏你的丑态。
t_en: Finally. And right on cue. Let your friends get a good look at how far you've fallen.

t_ch: 别急着动手，红色的帅哥。别看老身这样，在你动手之前，带这位音符小姐一起上路，还是绰绰有余。
t_en: Don't be so quick to charge in, red handsome. I might look frail, but I could still drag Miss Note down with me before you land a hit.

t_ch: 我为什么要为一个敌对的从者放下武器？让你们两个一起死掉，岂不是皆大欢喜？
t_en: And why would I spare an enemy Servant? Two birds with one stone sounds perfect to me.

t_ch: 在我看来，你不过也和葛瑞迪一样，是个虚张声势的空壳。
t_en: You seem like just another hollow loudmouth, like Grady.

t_ch: 也是，你们这群愚蠢的外乡人当然不了解，在匹诺康尼这片土地上，这个失去了名字的从者到底有多么强大。
t_en: Figures. You foolish outworlders don't realize just how powerful this nameless Servant can be in Penacony.

t_ch: 就当是公益演出好了，让你们好好品味Caster力量的冰山一角。
t_en: Call it a charitable performance. Let me give you a taste of what Caster can really do.

t_ch: 同谐的歌声，足以让所有生命心甘情愿地为我伴舞，成为我的使魔。至于你们，就在糖浆里溺死吧。
t_en: The voice of Harmony will compel all life to dance to my tune and become my familiars. And you? You'll drown in syrupy oblivion.

t_ch: 这可说不定啊。
t_en: We'll see about that.

t_ch: 用偷来的武器战斗的，终会被手中的武器所伤。如果我是你，就不会如此鲁莽地使用从音符小姐那儿抢来的力量。
t_en: Anyone who fights with a stolen weapon is bound to get hurt by it. If I were you, I'd think twice before swinging something you ripped off from Miss Note.

t_ch: 御主，你还好吧？
t_en: Master, are you all right?

t_ch: 正打算活动筋骨。
t_en: I was just about to do some stretches.

t_ch: 不，这种程度的小喽啰，还用不着劳烦御主你出手。
t_en: No need to trouble yourself with small fry like this, Master.

t_ch: Archer他恐吓我。
t_en: Archer's threatening me!

t_ch: Archer，你又在{NICKNAME}面前胡说了什么？
t_en: Archer, what nonsense are you feeding {NICKNAME} this time?

t_ch: 我没有——不是，现在是找大人告状的时候吗？
t_en: I didn't — wait, is this really the time to tattle to the grown-ups?

t_ch: 让我们并肩作战。
t_en: Let's fight side by side.

t_ch: 不，这种程度的小喽啰，还用不着劳烦御主你出手。
t_en: No need to trouble yourself with small fry like this, Master.

t_ch: 敢抢我这主演的戏？都给我上，谁能拿下他们，投资人给它报销一辈子的苏乐达！
t_en: Trying to steal the spotlight from the star!? Get them! Whoever takes them down gets a lifetime supply of SoulGlad from our investors!

t_ch: 为了苏乐达！
t_en: For SoulGlad!
